audit_items.proof_trace: The file contains pointer dereferences without null guards: `*thread` at L66/L69 in `ldap_pvt_thread_create` and `*data` at L190 in `ldap_pvt_thread_key_getdata`. However, these are the lowest-level GNU Pth threading wrappers; their callers within OpenLDAP always supply valid server-allocated buffers whose values are never derived from client-supplied LDAP PDU data. No external LDAP input flows into these pointer arguments, so the missing null guards represent a defensive-programming gap only, not an exploitable vulnerability.
